primer.markdown:
# One stylesheet, two themes

Dual-theme PDF generation in BARQUE is achieved with a single stylesheet that defines design tokens at the `:root` level and overrides them under a `[data-theme="parchment"]` selector. The renderer toggles a single attribute and re-runs WeasyPrint; the same Markdown produces either a dark or a light PDF without source-level forking. The lesson here is invariant: token-driven theming is reversible at runtime, deterministic at build time, and impossible to corrupt with one-off overrides if the token system is the only source of color.
primer.key_insight: Token-only theming makes both themes derivable from one source.

visual.alt: Vector equilibrium showing two theme states as mirrored vertices

interactive.success_criterion: Static side-by-side preview of dark + parchment theme renders without console errors.

questions.prompt: Where does the parchment theme override its tokens?
questions.explanation: Inside the [data-theme="parchment"] selector — siblings of :root, not children.
